titleEn: Australian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test
titleZh: 澳大利亚蔬菜农场实测两种除草技术
summaryZh: 澳大利亚有机蔬菜农场Busch Organics测试了两种前沿除草技术：丹麦Agrointelli公司的自主机器人平台Robotti，以及激光除草设备。该农场位于维多利亚州吉普斯兰，占地超过300英亩，已有百年历史。文章介绍了这些技术如何帮助有机农场减少人工除草依赖，提高效率，并可能重塑有机蔬菜种植方式。
featuredReason: 该新闻展示了自主机器人和激光除草技术在有机蔬菜种植中的实际应用案例，对智慧畜牧行业人士理解跨领域自动化技术趋势具有参考价值，尤其是机器人平台在精准农业中的潜力。
contentFull: Australian vegetable farm puts 2 weeding technologies to the test 24-06 | Crop solutions | ArticleThe first machine to arrive at Busch Organics was the Robotti, an autonomous implement carrier developed by Danish company Agrointelli. Photo: Busch OrganicsThe Mitchell River Flats near Bairnsdale in Gippsland, Australia, are not the first place you might expect to find agricultural robotics at work. But on the organic farm that Kane Busch’s great-grandfather established more than a century ago, the family has trialed technology that could reshape how organic vegetables are grown. Busch Organics spans more than 300 acres (121 hectares) of river flat country, 13 kilometres west of Bairnsdale at Hillside. translationZh:
位于澳大利亚吉普斯兰拜恩斯代尔附近的米切尔河滩，可能不是你最先想到能看到农业机器人作业的地方。但在这个由凯恩·布施的曾祖父在一个多世纪前建立的有机农场上，这个家族正在试验可能重塑有机蔬菜种植方式的技术。

author: René Groeneveld
contentHtml: Manual weeding costs Australian organic grower Busch Organics a significant amount of time and money. Field trials with autonomous hoeing and laser weeding have brought the company closer to a possible solution.
